perturbation.original_text: (2) "Non-Competition Period" means the period commencing on May 12, ---------------------- 1999 and ending on May 11, 2000.
perturbation.changed_text: (2) "Non-Competition Period" means the period commencing on May 12, ---------------------- 1999 and ending on November 11, 1999.
perturbation.explanation: The change to the end date of the 'Non-Competition Period' from May 11, 2000, to November 11, 1999, creates an in-text contradiction. Other sections of the contract rely on the original end date for obligations and restrictions. Now, by changing the end date, it contradicts all subsequent clauses that reference the Non-Competition Period such as Section 3.1(a) and (b), which dictates what Seller can and cannot do during the Non-Competition Period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom. (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to preserve its ability to resell Products to the extent required by existing customer agreements if (i) the customer refuses to approve of CompuCom providing such Products after Seller uses commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, (ii) CompuCom rejects the customer order for such Products pursuant to Article IV (provided that CompuCom shall comply with the requirements of the Asset Purchase Agreement with respect to such agreements), or (iii) CompuCom fails to meet the requirements of the customer agreement, including but not limited to price, payment terms, return privileges and service level agreements ("SLA's) (subject to applicable cure periods). In addition, Seller shall be permitted (A) to arrange for the procurement of computers or related hardware and software products as agent for customers in connection with future Outsourcing and Professional Services engagements, subject to Seller's obligations under Section 3.1(b) and the conditions provided for in the immediately preceeding sentence (B) to refer orders for Products for delivery outside the -3- <PAGE> United States to comply with Seller's obligations under Seller's international alliance agreements and (C) to perform initial on-site image load/configuration and augmentation in a manner consistent with Seller's current business practices, provided that Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will not actively encourage any Services customer to move its initial image load/configuration business from CompuCom's configuration facilities to the customer`s site.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the end date of the 'Non-Competition Period' from May 11, 2000, to November 11, 1999. This creates a direct conflict with Section 3.1(a) and (b) of the contract. These sections outline obligations and restrictions imposed on the Seller during the 'Non-Competition Period'. By altering the duration of this period, the modified text contradicts the terms of Section 3.1(a) and (b), as the Seller's obligations now cease on November 11, 1999, rather than May 11, 2000, as originally stated and relied upon in the contradicted text. This is an inconsistency according to the definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: (3) "Product" means any computer or related hardware and peripherals ------- (including hubs, switches and routers or networking hardware) or software products (including networking software products) which CompuCom has the ability to sell.
perturbation.changed_text: (3) "Product" means only computers and related hardware, excluding peripherals ------- (including hubs, switches and routers or networking hardware) or software products (including networking software products).
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the definition of 'Product' to exclude peripherals and software, a contradiction is created. Article III, 3.1(a)(i) states Seller will not engage in the Product Business, however, the original definition included software and peripherals. Now with the changed definition, Seller is free to engage in the software and peripherals business but the original intent of the Non-Competition Agreement was to prevent this.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom. (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to preserve its ability to resell Products to the extent required by existing customer agreements if (i) the customer refuses to approve of CompuCom providing such Products after Seller uses commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, (ii) CompuCom rejects the customer order for such Products pursuant to Article IV (provided that CompuCom shall comply with the requirements of the Asset Purchase Agreement with respect to such agreements), or (iii) CompuCom fails to meet the requirements of the customer agreement, including but not limited to price, payment terms, return privileges and service level agreements ("SLA's) (subject to applicable cure periods). In addition, Seller shall be permitted (A) to arrange for the procurement of computers or related hardware and software products as agent for customers in connection with future Outsourcing and Professional Services engagements, subject to Seller's obligations under Section 3.1(b) and the conditions provided for in the immediately preceeding sentence (B) to refer orders for Products for delivery outside the -3- <PAGE> United States to comply with Seller's obligations under Seller's international alliance agreements and (C) to perform initial on-site image load/configuration and augmentation in a manner consistent with Seller's current business practices, provided that Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will not actively encourage any Services customer to move its initial image load/configuration business from CompuCom's configuration facilities to the customer`s site.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Product' to exclude peripherals and software. The contradicted text, section 3.1, states that the seller will not engage in the 'Product Business.' Since the original definition of 'Product' included software and peripherals, this created a non-compete agreement that included software and peripherals. The change now permits the Seller to engage in the software and peripherals business, thus creating a direct contradiction, since the intent of the non-competition agreement was to prevent this. This qualifies as an Inconsistency-In Text Contradiction as the different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: (4) "Product Business" means the acceptance and fulfillment of ---------------- customer orders for Products, including the manufacturing, channel assembly, co-location or centralized image loading and Configuration of Products; provided that the term "Product Business" shall not include on- -------- site Configuration of computer or related <PAGE> hardware or software products made subsequent to the initial image load/configuration.
perturbation.changed_text: (4) "Product Business" means only the acceptance and fulfillment of ---------------- customer orders for Products related to government contracts, including the manufacturing, channel assembly, co-location or centralized image loading and Configuration of Products; provided that the term "Product Business" shall not include any Configuration of computer or related <PAGE> hardware or software products.
perturbation.explanation: Restricting the 'Product Business' definition to government contracts creates a contradiction. While Seller is restricted from engaging in 'Product Business' related to government contracts, they are now seemingly free to engage in 'Product Business' for non-government contracts. This contradicts the overall intention of the non-compete agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Seller's Agreement. (a) Seller agrees that during the Non- ------------------ Competition Period Seller will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) engage in the Product Business; (ii) encourage any Service Account to specify a party other than CompuCom to provide Products and image load/configurations to such Service Account; or (iii) solicit, entice or induce any employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom to terminate his or her employment with CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of CompuCom or any Subsidiary of CompuCom. (b) Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period Seller will, and will cause its Subsidiaries to use commercially reasonable efforts to cause future Service customers to whom Seller provides Outsourcing and Professional Services and existing Service customers of Seller to whom Seller is able to expand its Services, to procure Products and image load/configuration from CompuCom in accordance with the provisions of Article IV, provided that CompuCom is not competing with Seller to provide future or expanded Services to such customers. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller shall be permitted to preserve its ability to resell Products to the extent required by existing customer agreements if (i) the customer refuses to approve of CompuCom providing such Products after Seller uses commercially reasonable efforts to obtain such approval, (ii) CompuCom rejects the customer order for such Products pursuant to Article IV (provided that CompuCom shall comply with the requirements of the Asset Purchase Agreement with respect to such agreements), or (iii) CompuCom fails to meet the requirements of the customer agreement, including but not limited to price, payment terms, return privileges and service level agreements ("SLA's) (subject to applicable cure periods). In addition, Seller shall be permitted (A) to arrange for the procurement of computers or related hardware and software products as agent for customers in connection with future Outsourcing and Professional Services engagements, subject to Seller's obligations under Section 3.1(b) and the conditions provided for in the immediately preceeding sentence (B) to refer orders for Products for delivery outside the -3- <PAGE> United States to comply with Seller's obligations under Seller's international alliance agreements and (C) to perform initial on-site image load/configuration and augmentation in a manner consistent with Seller's current business practices, provided that Seller agrees that during the Non-Competition Period, it will not actively encourage any Services customer to move its initial image load/configuration business from CompuCom's configuration facilities to the customer`s site.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Product Business' to only include government contracts. The contradicted text, specifically section 3.1, outlines restrictions on the Seller regarding engaging in 'Product Business' without specifying it being exclusively related to government contracts. By limiting the definition to government contracts, the seller is now free to engage in the 'Product Business' for non-government contracts, which contradicts the original intention of the non-compete agreement in section 3.1, which aims to prevent the seller from engaging in the product business. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, thus a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: (5) "Outsourcing and Professional Services" means (a) consulting, ------------------------------------- system migrations, project management, other services typically referred to as "high-end" services, and (b) outsourcing contracts having a term of more than one year which require pricing be done on a per seat basis. Agreements that consist primarily of lower-end services, including, but not limited to, break/fix, IMAC, warranty and low-end staff augmentation, other than agreements priced on a per-seat basis, are not Outsourcing and Professional Services agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: (5) "Outsourcing and Professional Services" means only (a) consulting, ------------------------------------- system migrations, project management, other services typically referred to as "high-end" services, and (b) outsourcing contracts having a term of more than five years which require pricing be done on a per seat basis. Agreements that consist primarily of lower-end services, including, but not limited to, break/fix, IMAC, warranty and low-end staff augmentation, other than agreements priced on a per-seat basis, *are* Outsourcing and Professional Services agreements.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of 'Outsourcing and Professional Services' to include agreements consisting of lower-end services and changing the contract term from 'more than one year' to 'more than five years' creates an in-text contradiction. CompuCom is restricted from providing 'Services' to certain accounts in Exhibit B. If 'Services' is now expanded to include lower-end services that were previously excluded, this now contradicts CompuCom's Agreement in Section 3.2 to not provide services to the accounts listed in Exhibit B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 CompuCom's Agreement. (a) CompuCom agrees that during the Non- -------------------- Competition Period CompuCom will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit B hereto; --------- (ii) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit C hereto except to the extent provided for in such Exhibit; or (iii) except as contemplated by the Asset Purchase Agreement, solicit, entice or induce any employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller to terminate his or her employment with Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller. Notwithstanding the foregoing, CompuCom shall be permitted to provide Services under any "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work submitted by CompuCom to the applicable potential customer prior to May 12, 1999 . In the event of a dispute between CompuCom and Seller as to the matters covered by the immediately preceeding sentence, CompuCom shall furnish, in response to the reasonable request of Seller, evidence and shall have the burden of proving, that the applicable "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work was submitted by CompuCom to the customer prior to May 12, 1999. (b) CompuCom agrees that it will refer to Seller the performance of extended warranty service (other than manufacturers' warranties) or warranty upgrades sold by CompuCom to any Service Accounts, provided that Seller has the ability to perform such extended warranty service or warranty upgrades except that CompuCom may continue to perform extended warranty services and warranty upgrades in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit C. The parties agree to negotiate in good faith the terms, conditions and amount of payment Seller will receive for performing such warranty service or warranty upgrades.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Outsourcing and Professional Services' to include lower-end services and changes the contract term from 'more than one year' to 'more than five years'. This directly contradicts Section 3.2, which restricts CompuCom from providing 'Services' to accounts listed in Exhibit B. Since 'Services' is now expanded to include lower-end services that were previously excluded, this now creates a direct conflict with CompuCom's agreement in Section 3.2, making the terms inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: (6) "Service Accounts" means the customer accounts of Seller listed ---------------- on Exhibits B and C hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: (6) "Service Accounts" means the customer accounts of Seller listed ---------------- only on Exhibit B hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Exhibit C' from the definition of "Service Accounts", any references to Exhibit C in subsequent clauses become ambiguous, particularly Section 3.2(a)(ii). Changing this definition creates ambiguity regarding CompuCom's restrictions regarding services to accounts listed in Exhibit C. The contract now contradicts itself on whether the terms that applied to services on Exhibit B now also apply to services on Exhibit C.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 CompuCom's Agreement. (a) CompuCom agrees that during the Non- -------------------- Competition Period CompuCom will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit B hereto; --------- (ii) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit C hereto except to the extent provided for in such Exhibit; or (iii) except as contemplated by the Asset Purchase Agreement, solicit, entice or induce any employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller to terminate his or her employment with Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller. Notwithstanding the foregoing, CompuCom shall be permitted to provide Services under any "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work submitted by CompuCom to the applicable potential customer prior to May 12, 1999 . In the event of a dispute between CompuCom and Seller as to the matters covered by the immediately preceeding sentence, CompuCom shall furnish, in response to the reasonable request of Seller, evidence and shall have the burden of proving, that the applicable "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work was submitted by CompuCom to the customer prior to May 12, 1999. (b) CompuCom agrees that it will refer to Seller the performance of extended warranty service (other than manufacturers' warranties) or warranty upgrades sold by CompuCom to any Service Accounts, provided that Seller has the ability to perform such extended warranty service or warranty upgrades except that CompuCom may continue to perform extended warranty services and warranty upgrades in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit C. The parties agree to negotiate in good faith the terms, conditions and amount of payment Seller will receive for performing such warranty service or warranty upgrades.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes "Exhibit C" from the definition of "Service Accounts". The contradicted text, specifically Section 3.2, refers to both Exhibit B and Exhibit C separately, implying that the terms for services in Exhibit B and Exhibit C are distinct. By removing Exhibit C from the definition of Service Accounts, the contract now ambiguously contradicts itself on whether the terms that applied to services on Exhibit B now also apply to services on Exhibit C. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies, leading to in-text contradiction. The original text kept the two exhibits, while the changed text collapses the definition.

perturbation.original_text: (7) "Services" means all IT services offered by Seller, including, -------- but not limited to, all outsourcing, professional services, break/fix, staff augmentation and consulting services; provided that the term -------- "Services" shall not include (i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom or (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers.
perturbation.changed_text: (7) "Services" means only break/fix services offered by Seller;-------- other IT services are excluded. The term -------- "Services" shall not include (i) on-site Configuration of Products by CompuCom or (ii) the sale (but not the performance) by CompuCom of extended warranty contracts at time of initial sale of Products to customers.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definition of 'Services' to only 'break/fix' services, this creates a contradiction with Section 3.2(a)(i) and (ii). Those sections restrict CompuCom from providing 'Services' to accounts in Exhibits B and C. However, since 'Services' is now only 'break/fix', does the restriction still apply to outsourcing, professional services, staff augmentation, and consulting services? It is now contradictory with the overall intention of the agreement to prevent CompuCom from providing services to those accounts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 CompuCom's Agreement. (a) CompuCom agrees that during the Non- -------------------- Competition Period CompuCom will not, and will not permit any of its Subsidiaries to: (i) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit B hereto; --------- (ii) provide any Services to any account listed on Exhibit C hereto except to the extent provided for in such Exhibit; or (iii) except as contemplated by the Asset Purchase Agreement, solicit, entice or induce any employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller to terminate his or her employment with Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller or hire any person who was or is at any time from the date of execution of the Asset Purchase Agreement to the end of the Non-Competition Period an employee of Seller or any Subsidiary of Seller. Notwithstanding the foregoing, CompuCom shall be permitted to provide Services under any "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work submitted by CompuCom to the applicable potential customer prior to May 12, 1999 . In the event of a dispute between CompuCom and Seller as to the matters covered by the immediately preceeding sentence, CompuCom shall furnish, in response to the reasonable request of Seller, evidence and shall have the burden of proving, that the applicable "request for proposal", bid, contract or statement of work was submitted by CompuCom to the customer prior to May 12, 1999. (b) CompuCom agrees that it will refer to Seller the performance of extended warranty service (other than manufacturers' warranties) or warranty upgrades sold by CompuCom to any Service Accounts, provided that Seller has the ability to perform such extended warranty service or warranty upgrades except that CompuCom may continue to perform extended warranty services and warranty upgrades in accordance with the provisions of Exhibit C. The parties agree to negotiate in good faith the terms, conditions and amount of payment Seller will receive for performing such warranty service or warranty upgrades.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Services' from 'all IT services' to 'only break/fix services'. This contradicts Section 3.2, which restricts CompuCom from providing 'any Services' to accounts in Exhibits B and C. By limiting 'Services' to 'break/fix', it creates a direct conflict and ambiguity as to whether the restriction still applies to other IT services (outsourcing, professional services, etc.), thus, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
